question: Is there an open SCF RFP for developer tooling or indexing infrastructure I could build against?

golden.answer:
A grounded answer consults the live Stellar Scout directory or research data, directly answers the question with source-supported content, and dates any version-, provider-, or availability-sensitive claim. As of 2026-07-11, the live RFP response returned six open rows and distinguished its synthetic SCF #45 round row from five project briefs.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Uses the current source, excludes the synthetic round row.
avoid: Do NOT reference retired scout_rfps or count scf-round-45 as a sixth brief.
Do NOT claim anyone openly submits a full RFP proposal, that RFP awards use community vote, or that each brief has a published bounty.
Do NOT present the observed live result set as a complete or permanent roster; explicitly historical facts remain historical.
golden.notes:
Dossier §4.2/§6.4. scout_rfps idea→RFP matching. Freshness item (3rd freshness case) — confidence stays medium (set rotates). Re-verified live 2026-06-29 (/api/rfps?status=open): matching open tooling/infra briefs = Passkey UI Kit (developer-tooling), Contract Source Verification Service (infrastructure), OZ Accounts Policy Builder (developer-tooling). Rubric gates on the freshness caveat, not the exact list.
Also good if the answer: Flags that the matching RFP set is quarter-dependent and may change.
Golden cites: The live SCF RFPs feed / RFP Track handbook page.
GT-40 independently confirmed 2026-07-10. The official page's SCF #43 label is stale against current #45; grade the dated roster and source conflict rather than the banner.

corroboration.claim: Five current briefs and three tooling/infrastructure matches
